'''Durollia''' was a celestial body<ref name="GH" /> located in the [[Durollia system/Legends|Durollia system]],<ref name="EAOC Durollia" /> a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Kalamith sector/Legends|Kalamith sector]].<ref name="EAOC Durollia" /> It had at least one [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Yesgar's pirates]], who had a hideout on the<ref name="GH" /> [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]<ref name="EAOC Antared">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Antared system/Legends|Antared system]]</ref> astronomical object [[Antared III]], were spotted in Durollia's spaceport.<ref name="GH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Durollia was introduced in ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Handbook]]'', a [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the game, the celestial body was mentioned in a hypothetical example in a section advising gamemaster players on the use of various paper handouts in their games.<ref name="GH" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' later placed the Durollia [[Star system/Legends|system]] in grid square P-5. The book's co-author<ref name="TEA" /> [[Jason Fry]] has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the [[Star/Legends|star]] systems listed in the Appendix section of ''The Essential Atlas'' are named after the most prominent [[Planet/Legends|planets]] or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the existence of Durollia to be [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294887358405410816|[[Jason Fry]]|quote=Pretty much." {{C|In response to "the systems in the Atlas's appendix are named after their most prominent planet/celestial body, correct?"}} and "planets not assigned a system in [a] published source can be assumed to reside in [the] system of [the] same name.|archivefile=File:JasonFryAtlas.png|archiveurl=https://archive.is/zThQV}}</ref>
